“The ball mill works by rotating around its axis, causing impact and attrition between the balls and the powder particles.”
“Select the desired milling speed and duration. You may re-run the experiment with new settings later for comparison.”
“Now, the powder and stainless-steel balls are loaded into the mill. A 10:1 ratio ensures efficient impact energy transfer.”
Press the start button to begin the rotation
“Notice how prolonged milling results in finer, more homogeneous powder. Excessive milling, however, can generate heat and cause agglomeration.”
“Now, collect a small sample to measure particle size under a virtual microscope.”
“Use the stage micrometer to measure particle diameters. Record data for analysis.”
“The system automatically records 50 particle measurements and calculates the mean and standard deviation.”
“You can now perform the experiment again using a different milling speed or duration. This will help you compare how varying these parameters affects particle size reduction.”
“Here, you can visualize how increasing milling time and speed leads to progressive size reduction. Compare your results by re-running the experiment.”
“You’ve successfully completed the simulation. Milling parameters directly influence the extent of size reduction. Optimizing these ensures consistent product quality in pharmaceutical formulations.”
